report_name: New 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Worm Hits npm & PyPI in Major Supply Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat group attributed to the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' supply chain attack, which compromised over 170 packages across npm and PyPI by hijacking CI/CD pipelines to steal developer credentials and self-propagate.
threat_actors.goals: Widespread credential theft, targeting API keys, cloud service credentials, cryptocurrency wallets, and secrets for AI development tools.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The attack compromised the trust relationship between developers and the npm and PyPI package repositories by injecting malicious code into legitimate packages.

attack_patterns.name: Python
attack_patterns.description: The Mini Shai-Hulud worm executed malicious Python code when compromised PyPI packages were installed or used.

attack_patterns.name: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: The Mini Shai-Hulud worm executed malicious JavaScript code when compromised npm packages were installed or used.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The attackers stole a short-lived OIDC token from a GitHub Actions runner's memory to gain authentication to the npm registry for publishing malicious packages.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The worm used stolen API keys and tokens as valid accounts to authenticate to package registries (npm, PyPI) and publish new malicious versions, enabling its propagation.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The malware payload acts as a comprehensive credential-stealer, searching for and collecting secrets from various developer-related files and password stores, such as .npmrc and .git-credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The malware includes a destructive 'dead man's switch' payload (`rm -rf ~/`) that attempts to wipe the user's home directory.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attack injected malicious code into over 170 legitimate software packages on npm and PyPI by hijacking their CI/CD release pipelines.

malware.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
malware.description: A self-propagating worm that compromises npm and PyPI packages. It functions as a credential-stealer, targeting a wide range of developer secrets, and uses stolen credentials to publish new infected packages. It also contains a destructive payload to wipe the user's home directory.

tools.name: npm
tools.description: The npm command-line tool was abused by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm to publish malicious new versions of compromised packages.

tools.name: twine
tools.description: The twine command-line tool was likely abused by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm to upload malicious packages to the PyPI repository.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-45321
vulnerabilities.description: A critical vulnerability related to the compromise of the TanStack project's build process, which allowed the Mini Shai-Hulud worm to be published with valid provenance attestations.

campaigns.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
campaigns.description: A sophisticated, automated supply chain attack campaign by TeamPCP targeting open-source software repositories like npm and PyPI. The campaign uses a self-propagating worm to steal developer credentials and spread to more packages.

identities.name: TanStack
identities.description: A high-profile open-source project whose CI/CD release pipeline was hijacked by TeamPCP to publish 84 malicious package versions as part of the Mini Shai-Hulud attack.

identities.name: Mistral AI
identities.description: A high-profile AI company whose packages were compromised in the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack.

identities.name: UiPath
identities.description: A high-profile software company whose packages were compromised in the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The npm package repository was targeted in the Mini Shai-Hulud attack, with numerous malicious packages published to it.

identities.name: PyPI
identities.description: The PyPI package repository was targeted in the Mini Shai-Hulud attack, with numerous malicious packages published to it.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The GitHub Actions platform was the environment where the CI/CD pipeline compromise occurred, involving cache poisoning and OIDC token theft.

infrastructure.name: Credential Exfiltration Endpoints
infrastructure.description: Paste sites such as paste.bing and dpaste.com were suggested as potential exfiltration endpoints for credentials stolen by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm.

indicators.name: Mini Shai-Hulud Potential Credential Exfiltration
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Mini Shai-Hulud Potential Credential Exfiltration DNS Query
id: 2b1a9c8d-e3f4-4a5b-8c6d-7e8f9a0b1c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to paste sites mentioned as potential exfiltration endpoints for the Mini Shai-Hulud worm.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/mini-shai-hulud-worm-compromises.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'paste.bing'
      - 'dpaste.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to paste sites that were suggested as potential exfiltration endpoints for the Mini Shai-Hulud worm.

indicators.name: Mini Shai-Hulud Destructive Payload Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Mini Shai-Hulud Destructive Command
id: 8c7b6a5d-4e3f-4d2c-1b0a-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of 'rm -rf ~/', a destructive command used as a 'dead man's switch' by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/mini-shai-hulud-worm-compromises.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'rm -rf ~/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'rm -rf ~/', a destructive command used as a 'dead man's switch' by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm.

indicators.name: Mini Shai-Hulud Credential File Discovery
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Mini Shai-Hulud Credential File Access
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects processes accessing common developer credential files targeted by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/mini-shai-hulud-worm-compromises.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1552
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '/.aws/credentials'
      - '/.npmrc'
      - '/.git-credentials'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to common developer credential files that are targeted by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm for theft.

indicators.name: Anomalous Package Publishing Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Mini Shai-Hulud Package Publishing
id: f0e9d8c7-b6a5-4f3e-2d1c-0b9a8f7e6d5c
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of 'npm publish' or 'twine upload', which could indicate compromise by the Mini Shai-Hulud worm outside of a normal developer workflow.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/mini-shai-hulud-worm-compromises.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'npm publish'
      - 'twine upload'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of package publishing commands which could be indicative of the Mini Shai-Hulud worm's propagation mechanism.
